text: Deadlines can be stressful, but handling them effectively is a valuable skill. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps and setting personal mini-deadlines. Prioritization is also key: focus on urgent or important work first rather than getting distracted. If a project feels overwhelming, practice scope management by clarifying what is actually required and avoiding unnecessary additions. Finally, communication with your team or supervisor is essential—if you foresee a delay, inform them early. Being honest allows others to adjust their plans or offer support. With these strategies, you can meet deadlines calmly.
